highlights:
# Technical Lead, Principal
...
The Data Solutions Architecture Team at Pacific Gas & Electric Company is responsible for driving long-term, enterprise-wide data solutions, target state architecture, and overall excellence of data, analytics and information applications. The team owns the strategy, roadmap, and accompanying standards that enable effective use of data and information, and to develop analytics maturity at PG&E.
...
The Principal Technical Lead is responsible for setting technical direction, driving architectural excellence, and steering complex data and platform engineering initiatives across multiple product teams. This role extends and elevates the data platform/product architecture by: Defining cross -domain architecture strategy and influencing enterprise roadmaps Leading large -scale, multiteam solution design Guiding product and engineering leaders on technical feasibility, standards, and platform boundaries Acting as a thought leader across IT and the business, shaping long -term data platform evolution
...
The Principal Technical Lead operates with significant autonomy, provides mentorship to other architects and technical leads, and ensures technology decisions align with enterprise scale needs. This position is central to delivering forward-thinking data products that are reliable, scalable, and industry leading.
...
The Principal Technical Lead will report to the Senior Manager of Data Solutions Architecture in the Data Analytics & Insights department of Information Technology.
...

results.title: Principal Quantitative Analyst - Energy Policy & Procurement | Hirevector
highlights: a highly motivated Principal Analyst with experience in electric portfolio forecasting analytics, generation portfolio simulation modeling, scenario analysis, data visualization, data management, utility business model, and business process management. The Analyst will work closely with stakeholders to develop clean energy, emissions and reliability position forecasts associated with PG&E’s generation portfolio to ensure PG&E meets ambitious climate and energy reliability goals. The Analyst will generate high-value business insights by identifying, framing, and executing analyses related to the business impacts of forecasts, including driving decisions on PG&E’s long-term energy procurement to reach 100% greenhouse gas-free (GHG-Free) by 2045. As part of this work the Analyst will systematically evaluate and recommend improvements to the forecasting process, models, inputs, and platforms for production and delivery of forecasts, and will work closely with commercial and strategy teams across the organization. The candidate will be capable of developing and managing cross-functional projects, products, or processes. The candidate will also have excellent written and verbal communication skills with demonstrated experience informing and influencing senior leadership and external stakeholders. This position is hybrid, working from your remote office and your assigned work location based on business need. The work location is Oakland, CA. PG&E is providing the salary range that can reasonably be expected for this position at the time of the job posting. This salary range is specific to the locality of the job. The actual salary paid to an individual will be based on multiple factors, including, but not limited to, internal equity, specific skills, education, licenses or certifications, experience, market value, and geographic location. The decision will be made on a case-by-case basis related to these factors. This job is also eligible to participate in PG&E’s discretionary incentive compensation programs. A reasonable salary range is: Bay Area: $144,000.00 to $213,400.00 Responsibilities • Forecasting : Develop generation and compliance forecasts for PG&E’s clean energy, emissions and reliability positions. This includes developing and managing simulation modeling to account for identified uncertainties that impact PG&E’s electric generation portfolio. Ensure that the forecast is systematically and appropriately integrated into long-term planning strategy as well as policy and planning processes. Conduct research to inform/validate inputs, assumptions, and scenarios. Conduct market and policy research to identify critical uncertainties and inform the key inputs, assumptions, and the range of scenarios to be forecasted. Collaborate closely with commercial and strategy teams to drive informed commercial decision making. • Market Intelligence and Assessment: Perform market assessment of supply-side and demand side energy resources for electricity. • Analysis : Produce analytics that guide PG&E’s resource, infrastructure, and strategic planning as well as public policy position development. The successful candidate will have a broad understanding of the California utility regulatory context and PG&E’s business model to help contextualize the forecasts. Communicate results to client organizations through effective data visualization and presentation materials. • Continuous Improvement : Drive continuous improvement to increase the efficiency, persuasiveness, and pervasiveness of electric portfolio forecasting. Provide support for the development and implementation of a roadmap to systematically identify improvements to the forecasting processes, analytics, inputs, and capabilities. Research and document forecasting best practices, client use-cases, product specifications, analytical methods, processes, and platforms for production and delivery of forecasts. • Policy Advocacy : Assist in developing analysis and

farms located in Sweden and Finland. Forecasts are generated for all of 2016 and are evaluated against historical data from that year for each of the 24 wind farms. The proposed models are successfully implemented into the .NET framework of Vitec Software’s Aiolos Forecast Studio, which is widely used in the Northern and Western Europe. Vitec’s Aiolos wind power forecast model is based on an ensemble of numerical weather prediction models and adaptive statistical machine learning algorithms. The proposed models are found to have significantly lower mean absolute error and root mean squared error compared to the Aiolos model and autoregressive model benchmarks. The improved short term wind power forecast will inform operation and trading decisions and translate to significant reductions in balancing costs for Vitecs customers. The improvement is valued at as much as between 9.4 million Euros to 42.3 million Euros in reduced balancing costs. Spatio-temporal aspects for wind power forecasting is shown to continue to be promising for improving current state-of-the-art wind power forecasting. Show less

results.title: Avi Shapiro
highlights:
Data Scientist | Climate Tech | Machine Learning | AI | Analytics |
...
Data Scientist at [Pacific Gas and Electric Company](https://www.linkedin.com/company/pg%26ampe)
...
I am a data scientist with nearly a decade of experience applying advanced analytics and visualization, machine learning, and data engineering to solve real-world problems. With a PhD in Applied Mathematics and a background as a university lecturer, I bring a rigorous academic foundation combined with practical industry expertise. My professional focus is on leveraging data science to address critical challenges in the climate tech sector, particularly in renewable energy, smart grids, distributed energy resources, and battery storage systems. I am deeply motivated by the opportunity to contribute to a more sustainable and energy-resilient future through data-driven innovation. Throughout my career, I have built and deployed end-to-end solutions, from careful data collection and annotation strategies, rigorous model validation, and scalable deployment. My approach is rooted in my broad experience, thoughtful communication, and a commitment to continuous improvement. I bring not only technical proficiency but also a collaborative mindset and a drive for efficiency in both code and process. I am seeking a full-time opportunity with a mission-driven organization, where I can apply my expertise in data science to advance impactful work in the energy and climate domains. Please reach out with advice, opportunities, or questions. Current domain interests: Distributed Energy Resource Management, Virtual Power Plants, smart grid, systems thinking Current technical interests: Data Engineering (Terraform, Airflow, vector and graph databases), climate-aware computing, Gurobi, GenAI, FastAPI, IoT Strengths: machine learning, signal processing, data analysis and visualization, statistics, Python (Jupyter, pandas, seaborn, sklearn, xgboost, numpy, scipy, shap, pytorch), deep learning, R (tidyverse, ggplot2), mathematical modeling, linear algebra, differential equations, data pipelines, MySQL, teaching, game theory, network science, simulation, AWS (SageMaker, Glue, Redshift, CloudFormation, Kinesis), Docker

I turn complex AI challenges into business wins—whether it’s building GPT-powered chatbots that don’t hallucinate, training YOLO models to spot rogue insulators, or automating data pipelines so you can focus on the fun stuff (like coffee breaks). With 6+ years of experience, I thrive where tech meets real-world impact. What I Bring to the Table: 🔸 AI That Works: Fine-tuned LLMs, RAG systems, and computer vision models deployed on Azure for energy, aviation, and education clients. 🔸 Full-Stack Execution: From prototyping in PyTorch to Dockerized deployments—no "but it works on my laptop" surprises. 🔸 Client Whisperer: Translating tech-speak into ROI—whether in pre-sales demos, Agile sprints, or post-deployment troubleshooting. Why Work Together? ✨ Reliability: Green Card holder with a track record of sticking around! ✨ Certified Geekery: Microsoft Azure AI Engineer, Excel wizard, SQL pro—because credentials matter, but results matter more. ✨ Human-Centered Leadership: DE&I advocate, mentor for women in tech, and contributor to media pieces on AI innovation and workplace equity. When I’m Not Coding: 🌱 Brainstorming ways to merge AI with sustainability. 🎤 Attending and speaking at global conferences. 🤖 Low-key plotting to revive my college project. results.title: Senior AI/ML Engineer at Jobgether - United State | Visa Sponsorship Jobs
highlights:
# Senior AI/ML Engineer
...
We are seeking a highly skilled AI/ML Engineer to develop advanced AI and ML solutions for the energy and utility sector. The ideal candidate will have a strong background in machine learning engineering, AI systems design, and large-scale data-driven decision-making. The role requires collaboration with cross-functional teams to design, build, and deploy scalable machine learning systems.
...
Collaborate with cross-functional teams to design, develop, and deploy scalable AI and machine learning solutions integrated into production systems
...
Build reusable Python libraries and engineering components to support ML pipelines, data processing workflows, and AI system development
...
Python AWS Azure GCP Databricks LangChain LangGraph LlamaIndex Hugging Face AutoGen
...
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for an AI / ML Engineer in the United States.This role sits at the intersection of machine learning engineering, AI systems design, and large-scale data-driven decision-making, focused on solving complex challenges in the energy and utility sector. You will contribute to the development of advanced AI and ML solutions that combine predictive modeling, optimization, and generative AI to support critical infrastructure and sustainability goals. Working closely with data scientists and software engineers, you will help design, build, and deploy scalable machine learning systems that power real-world applications. The environment is highly collaborative and innovation-driven, with a strong emphasis on applied AI, production readiness, and measurable impact. You will work across diverse use cases including forecasting, geospatial analysis, and intelligent decision systems. This is a high-impact role where your work directly supports the modernization and efficiency of energy systems.Accountabilities
...
- Collaborate with cross-functional teams to design, develop, and deploy scalable AI and machine learning solutions integrated into production systems.
...
- Build reusable Python libraries and engineering components to support ML pipelines, data processing workflows, and AI system development.
...
- Contribute to the design and implementation of modern AI architectures, including RAG systems, LLM integrations, and agent-based workflows.
...
- Support database design and optimization, including writing efficient queries for large-scale data processing and retrieval.
...
- Translate complex machine learning and AI requirements into structured technical tasks and actionable development plans.
...
- Stay current with advancements in machine learning engineering, generative AI, and AI orchestration, integrating best practices into workflows.
...
- Master’s degree in Computer Science, Data Science, Software Engineering, or related field (PhD preferred).
...
- 7+ years of experience building, deploying, and maintaining machine learning systems in production environments.
...
- Strong proficiency in Python, including experience building reusable packages and scalable data pipelines.
...
- Hands-on experience with cloud platforms such as AWS, Azure, or GCP, including CI/CD and containerized deployments.
...
- Solid understanding of ML system design, MLOps principles, and end-to-end model lifecycle management.
...
- Experience with distributed computing frameworks and relational/non-relational databases.
...
- Familiarity with Databricks and large-scale data processing environments.
...
- Experience working with generative AI frameworks such as LangChain, LangGraph, LlamaIndex, Hugging Face, or AutoGen.
...
- Exposure to agentic AI systems, AI orchestration, or LLM-based decision workflows is highly desirable.
...
- Nice to have: experience in the utility, energy, or power sector and strong Databricks expertise.
...

highlights:

...
I thrive at the intersection of data, innovation and business. With over 6 years of experience in analytics, I’ve built scalable data solutions that power decision-making, streamline operations, and drive measurable impact across retail, finance, and tech consulting. Currently, I wear many hats including data analyst, data engineer, and data scientist- depending on what the problem demands. My journey began with designing intuitive dashboards that made complex KPIs actionable for business teams. Over time, that evolved into architecting robust data pipelines and tackling large-scale data integration challenges. What energizes me most is working cross-functionally. Whether it’s partnering with product managers to define success metrics or collaborating with engineering teams to optimize pipelines, I enjoy being the bridge between technical complexity and business clarity. I’ve led projects that modernized reporting systems, automated analytics workflows, and supported C-suite decision-making through scalable dashboards and self-serve data products. I take pride in building products that are not just technically savvy but also aligned with user needs. Outside of work, I enjoy writing about data & analytics engineering through my Substack newsletter. If you’re building something exciting in data, especially around analytics engineering, AI products- I’d love to connect.

results.title: Director, Artificial Intelligence & Machine Learning | Wfh
highlights:
Director, Artificial Intelligence & Machine Learning | Wfh
...
# Director, Artificial Intelligence & Machine Learning
...
### About the role:
...
Angi is seeking an exceptional Director, Artificial Intelligence & Machine Learning to be a driving force behind Angi's transformation. The role is to be a leader in our Artificial Intelligence team, tackling challenges such as integrating Angi's search with LLM Providers like OpenAI GPT and Google Gemini. This is a leadership role, leading a team of dedicated machine learning engineers to both develop ML prototypes and then take them to production. In addition to being a leader, we're also looking for someone who can dive into tech and get their hands dirty to solve some of Angi's biggest technical challenges.
...
- Leadership: Lead and grow a team of talented machine learning engineers, providing guidance, mentorship, and support to foster professional growth. For our most complex challenges, you will dive into the tech to work through solutioning alongside your team.
...
- LLM Deployment and Optimization: Oversee the fine-tuning and deployment of LLMs, including Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) in an agentic framework, with a focus on optimizing model performance and scalability to enhance user experience and drive business results.
...
- Integration with 3rd Party AI APIs: Oversee the integration and management of AI tools and third-party solutions, including OpenAI GPT, Google Gemini, Meta AI, and Salesforce AI, to enhance user experience and business performance.
...
- Engineering & Product Collaboration: Partner with engineering, product, and design teams to build robust, scalable real-time APIs, and work on the entire development process to seamlessly integrate AI solutions into our products.
...
- Innovation: Foster innovation within the team, exploring new approaches and techniques to solve complex business problems.
...
- Who you are
...
- You have a Master's or Ph.D. in a computer science or quantitative field (e.g., Computer Science, Statistics, Mathematics, or related fields).
- You have 10+ years of experience in machine learning & engineering, with a focus on large-scale distributed application architecture for deploying ML models to production.
- You are an expert in machine learning deployment, API development, and real-time ML workloads.
- You are proficient in Python and comfortable diving deep into technical challenges alongside your team.
...
- You have excellent communication skills with the ability to convey complex technical concepts to non-technical stakeholders.
...
Our hiring process may utilize artificial intelligence (AI) tools to assist in candidate screening and assessment. Our AI tools are designed to complement, not replace, human decision-making.

results.title: Venkat Nikhil K
highlights:
Gen AI Engineer
...
Senior Gen AI Engineer and Lead engineer with 10+ years of experience designing, building, and optimizing LLM-enabled data platforms across AWS, GCP, Azure, and Snowflake environments. Strong expertise in Big Data ecosystems including Hadoop, Spark, Hive, Kafka, Airflow, and dbt, with a focus on building AI/LLM-ready data architectures. Proven experience developing end-to-end ELT/ETL pipelines for structured, semi-structured, and unstructured data, enabling Generative AI, RAG-based analytics, and intelligent data applications at enterprise scale. Hands-on experience with Snowflake data warehousing, including Snowpipe, Streams, Tasks, performance tuning, cost optimization, data governance, and secure data access for AI workloads. Advanced proficiency in Python, PySpark, SQL, and Unix scripting, with experience building LLM-integrated pipelines, AI-driven validation frameworks, and intelligent automation workflows. Extensive experience with workflow orchestration using Apache Airflow, Cloud Composer, and dbt Cloud, supporting AI-driven data processing and real-time analytics. Collaborated with data scientists to productionize AI/ML models and enable LLM-powered applications, supporting both real-time and batch inference pipelines. Expertise in data ingestion from RDBMS, APIs, and streaming platforms using Kafka, Sqoop, Flume, and Pub/Sub, building event-driven architectures for AI and LLM use cases. Experience building real-time and batch processing pipelines using Spark, Dataflow, and Kafka Streams, enabling low-latency data delivery for AI/LLM systems. Strong understanding of data quality, governance, and AI-ready validation frameworks, ensuring reliable and audit-compliant datasets. Built and optimized data pipelines to support AI/ML and LLM workloads, ensuring high-quality, scalable, and low-latency feature delivery. Proven ability to collaborate with business stakeholders, offshore teams, and cross-functional engineering teams to deliver AI-enabled and Agentic AI-driven data solutions, leveraging Python, AWS Bedrock, LangChain, LangGraph, and prompt engineering. Experience across healthcare, finance, insurance, life sciences, and ad-tech domains, delivering LLM-integrated data platforms and Agentic AI workflows supporting advanced analytics and intelligent data applications.

results.title: Principal Machine Learning Engineer- LLM Fine-tuning and Optimization | CareerSync
highlights:
Principal Machine Learning Engineer- LLM Fine-tuning and Optimization | CareerSync
...
# Principal Machine Learning Engineer- LLM Fine-tuning and Optimization
...
Machine Learning and Artificial Intelligence are at the heart of the Airbnb product. From Trust to Payments, and from Customer Service to Marketing we rely on ML to ensure that guests and hosts have the best possible experience with Airbnb.
...
The CS AI product team is responsible for driving CSxAI (Customer Support x Artificial Intelligence) initiatives by adopting the Generative AI technologies to enable an intelligent, scalable and exceptional service experience. The team develops and enhances various AI models, ML services and tools including LLM fine-tuning, alignment and optimization, RAG/Search, LLM evaluation and testing automation, feedback-based learning and guardrail for a wide range of applications in Airbnb.
...
As a principal machine learning engineer, you will be responsible for fine-tuning state-of-the-art LLMs for diverse use cases while optimizing models for high-performance deployment on Airbnb’s ML Infrastructure. You will partner with product managers, software engineers, data scientists and operation teams to brainstorm, design and develop AI products such as AI Assistant, Autonomous agent, recommendation, travel planning, and many more products that make meaningful impacts in the world of travel.
...
- Work with large scale structured and unstructured data; explore, experiment, build and continuously improve foundation models for Airbnb product, business and operational use cases.
...
- Create a multi-year tech roadmap that enables our team to stay on the leading edge of the rapidly evolving AI landscape and leverage the best in class technologies to deliver customer benefits.
...
- Continuously evaluate recent and upcoming large foundational models, ensuring the selection and refinement of the highest quality models for enhanced performance and efficiency.
...
- Hands-on prototype, develop and productionize LLM models and pipelines at scale, including both batch and real-time use cases.
...
- Drive key AI architectural decisions for products, and contribute to Airbnb’s ML platform architecture and strategy.
...
- Minimum Qualifications:
...
- PhD in Computer Science, Machine Learning, Mathematics, Statistics, or related technical field.
...
- 10+ years of experience with developing machine learning models and products at scale from inception to business impact.
...
- Programming experience in Python and hands-on experience with frameworks such as PyTorch.
...
- Proven record of training, fine tuning, optimizing models and inference run-time
...
- Post-training experience in areas like data processing for fine-tuning; responsible LLMs; LLM alignment; reinforcement learning; efficient training and inference; language model evaluation; and/or multilingual and multimodal modeling.
...
- Or specialized experience in runtime optimizations, model quantization, compression, on-device inference, GPU inference, pytorch, kernel development
...
- Preferred Qualifications:
...
- PhD in AI, machine learning, data science, or related technical fields.
